title: Three times scared
summary: A retired pilot watches planes with her twelve-year-old grandson at Amsterdam Schiphol. He asks if she was ever scared.

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: Lars, look at that one. The white plane with the blue line.

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: Yes. KL791. It is going to São Paulo. It always leaves at eleven.

body.speaker: Lars
body.text: How do you know the numbers?

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: I flew that route for sixteen years.

body.speaker: Lars
body.text: When you were the pilot, were you ever scared?

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: Once over the Atlantic, in 1989. Bad weather and one slow engine. Once in 2001, when a passenger was very sick and we had to land in Lisbon. Once in 2011, my last year. A young pilot in the second seat made a mistake. He was so sorry. I told him 'Now you will not make that mistake for twenty-five years.'

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: No. I was that pilot in 1978.

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: Everybody does. The captain over me was kind. She did the same for me. I did the same for him. That is how planes stay safe. Not by no mistakes. By kind captains.

body.speaker: Lars
body.text: Grandma, will I fly planes one day?

body.speaker: Mrs Larsen
body.text: I do not know. You will know in about ten years. Now, the white plane is leaving. It will turn right and go up.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple — irregular verbs
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A2 dùng nhiều động từ bất quy tắc ở quá khứ. Trong bài: 'I flew that route for sixteen years.' (fly → flew). 'I told him…' (tell → told). 'She did the same for me.' (do → did). 'I made a mistake.' (make → made). 'He was sorry.' (be → was). Không có quy tắc chung — phải nhớ từng cặp. Các động từ này thường được dùng trong kể chuyện.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A2 introduces many irregular past forms. The dialogue: 'I flew that route for sixteen years.' (fly → flew). 'I told him…' (tell → told). 'She did the same for me.' (do → did). 'I made a mistake.' (make → made). 'He was sorry.' (be → was). There is no general rule — you must memorise each pair. These verbs are very common in narration.

translationVi:
Bà Larsen: Lars, nhìn chiếc kia. Chiếc trắng có sọc xanh.
Lars: KLM ạ?
Bà Larsen: Đúng. KL791. Bay đi São Paulo. Luôn cất cánh lúc mười một giờ.
Lars: Sao bà biết số?
Bà Larsen: Bà bay đường đó mười sáu năm.
Lars: Khi bà là phi công, bà có bao giờ sợ không?
Bà Larsen: Có. Ba lần.
Lars: Chỉ ba lần thôi ạ?
Bà Larsen: Một lần qua Đại Tây Dương, năm 1989. Thời tiết xấu và một động cơ chạy chậm. Một lần năm 2001, khi một hành khách rất ốm và bà phải hạ cánh ở Lisbon. Một lần năm 2011, năm cuối. Một phi công trẻ ở ghế hai mắc lỗi. Cậu ấy rất xin lỗi. Bà nói với cậu: 'Giờ trong hai mươi lăm năm tới cậu sẽ không mắc lại lỗi đó.'
Lars: Bà có giận không?
Bà Larsen: Không. Bà chính là phi công đó năm 1978.
Lars: Bà cũng từng mắc lỗi?
Bà Larsen: Ai cũng vậy. Cơ trưởng trên bà tử tế. Cô ấy làm thế với bà. Bà làm thế với cậu ấy. Đó là cách máy bay an toàn. Không phải nhờ không có lỗi. Nhờ cơ trưởng tử tế.
Lars: Bà ơi, sau này cháu có lái máy bay không?
Bà Larsen: Bà không biết. Khoảng mười năm nữa cháu sẽ biết. Giờ, chiếc trắng đang đi rồi. Nó sẽ rẽ phải và lên cao.

questions.explanation: They are watching the white KLM plane.
questions.hint: The setting is suggested by the planes.

questions.prompt: What is the plane's flight number, and where is it going?
questions.explanation: 'KL791. It is going to São Paulo.'

questions.prompt: How long did she fly the route?
questions.explanation: 'I flew that route for sixteen years.'

questions.prompt: How many times was she scared in her career?

options: Bad weather over the Atlantic; one slow engine.
A young pilot made a mistake.
questions.explanation: Mrs Larsen lists the three scares.

questions.prompt: Where did she have to land in 2001?
What did she tell the young pilot in 2011?
options: 'Now you will not make that mistake for twenty-five years.'

questions.prompt: Why was she not angry with the young pilot?
options: Because she was that pilot in 1978 — she had made the same kind of mistake.
Because the captain told her not to be.

questions.prompt: How does she say planes stay safe?
options: Not by no mistakes — by kind captains.
questions.explanation: 'Not by no mistakes. By kind captains.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: Safety in a long career depends on a chain of kindness passed forward — not on the absence of mistakes.
Young pilots should not be hired.
questions.explanation: Mrs Larsen's 'I was that pilot' and the chain she describes are the point.
questions.hint: Notice the chain: 1978 captain → her → 2011 young pilot.

cloze.template:
Mrs Larsen: Lars, look at that one. The white plane with the blue line.
Lars: KLM?
Mrs Larsen: Yes. KL791. It is going to São Paulo. It always leaves at {{b1}}.
Lars: How do you know the numbers?
Mrs Larsen: I {{b2}} that route for sixteen years.
Lars: When you were the pilot, were you ever scared?
Mrs Larsen: Yes. {{b3}} times.
Lars: Only three?
Mrs Larsen: Once over the {{b4}}, in 1989. Bad weather and one slow engine. Once in 2001, when a passenger was very sick and we had to land in {{b5}}. Once in 2011, my last year. A young pilot in the second seat made a {{b6}}. He was so sorry. I told him 'Now you will not make that mistake for twenty-five years.'
Lars: Were you angry?
Mrs Larsen: No. I was that pilot in {{b7}}.
Lars: You also made a mistake?
Mrs Larsen: Everybody does. The {{b8}} over me was kind. She did the same for me. I did the same for him. That is how planes stay {{b9}}. Not by no mistakes. By kind captains.
Lars: Grandma, will I fly planes one day?
Mrs Larsen: I do not know. You will know in about {{b10}} years. Now, the white plane is leaving. It will turn right and go up.

blanks.explanation: The captain over me was kind.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mrs Larsen says safety is built by 'kind captains' who pass the same kindness forward, not by people who never make mistakes. Is there a place in your life — work, family — where you received a kind 'captain' moment? Have you passed it on to someone newer than you?
